name: User Access Review
description: User access to systems and data is periodically reviewed to ensure access remains appropriate and aligned with each individual's role. User access reviews reduce the risk of unauthorised or excessive access by confirming that only authorised individuals retain access and that access is revoked when no longer required.

attributes.text: Access reviews are performed on a periodic basis (e.g. quarterly)
testable_criteria: Is there evidence that a user access review was performed for the period under review?
Was the access review performed at the defined frequency (e.g. quarterly)?
Is the date the review was performed documented?
Did the review occur within the expected period (no missed or overdue review cycles)?

attributes.text: Access is reviewed and approved by an appropriate system or data owner
testable_criteria: Was the access review performed/approved by an identified individual?
Is the reviewer/approver an appropriate system or data owner for the system in scope?
Is there documented evidence of the reviewer's approval (e.g. sign-off, signature, ticket)?
Was the approval performed by someone independent of the access being reviewed (approver is not self-approving their own access)?

attributes.text: Inappropriate or excessive access identified during the review is remediated in a timely manner
testable_criteria: Were any instances of inappropriate or excessive access identified during the review documented?
IF inappropriate or excessive access was identified THEN is there evidence the access was remediated (e.g. removed or modified)?
Was the remediation completed in a timely manner following identification?
Is there evidence of the remediation date to confirm timeliness?
